project.objective: Who was the father of Jan Gallo, born in 1893 in Šútovo, Turany parish, Slovakia (then Suttó, Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary)?

questions.question: What does the Turány parish baptismal register record for Jan Gallo, born 1893 in Šútovo (Suttó), Turócz County, say about his father?
questions.rationale: Hungary did not implement civil registration until October 1895, so the 1893 birth falls entirely within the church-record era. The Turány (Turany) Roman Catholic parish registers are the primary source for baptisms in Šútovo/Suttó at this date and should directly name the father. FamilySearch holds digitized Hungarian/Slovak Catholic registers covering this region. This is the highest-value gatekeeper question: a positive baptismal entry resolves the objective in a single step.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Two independent original baptismal register entries from the Turány (Turany) Catholic parish — the 1893 baptism of Jan Gallo (ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R) and the 1895 baptism of sibling Ondrej Gallo (ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS) — both name Andrej/Ondrej Gallo as father and Katarina Križko/Kricko as mother. These are structurally separate, contemporaneously recorded register entries with different informant occasions, constituting independent documentary confirmation. A marriage record search across the indexed portion of the same collection returned no Gallo–Križko marriage entry, consistent with the record being in the unindexed portion of the register; this gap is documented and does not conflict with the established parentage. No conflicting source names a different father. Overturn risk is low.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — the 1893 Turány baptism directly names 'Andrej Gallo' as father of Jan Gallo (primary, direct evidence). The 1895 sibling baptism independently corroborates the same father and mother. The research question is answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Yes — FamilySearch collection 1554443 (Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935) searched for baptisms (pli_001, pli_003) and marriages (pli_002). Name variants Andrej/Ondrej and Križko/Kricko tried. MyHeritage fallback and 1869 census browse were contingency items not needed given the direct evidence found.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Yes — both src_001 and src_002 are classified 'original'; they are digital images of the contemporaneously recorded Turány Catholic parish register. The FamilySearch index was used only as a finding aid.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Yes — two independent sources: the 1893 Jan Gallo baptism and the 1895 Ondrej Gallo baptism. These are separate register entries two years apart, different events, different baptized individuals, different informant occasions. Same parents presented both times, but the acts of recording are independent.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — both records are original (church register images) with primary information quality. The baptismal entries were recorded contemporaneously by the officiating priest from information supplied by the presenting parents.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Yes — no source names a different father for Jan Gallo. The Andrej/Ondrej name variant and Križko/Kricko surname variant are documented in assertion informant_bias_notes. The absence of the Gallo–Križko marriage in indexed records is documented as a gap (log_003) but creates no conflict with the parentage evidence.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low — both decisive record types (baptismal register for the subject and a sibling) have been searched and agree. No unexamined record type is likely to name a different father. The unindexed Gallo–Križko marriage record and the browse-only 1869 census could potentially add context but are not expected to contradict the two concordant original baptismal entries.

items.jurisdiction: Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary (now Turany, Martin district, Slovakia)
items.rationale: Hungary did not have civil registration until October 1895, so Jan Gallo's 1893 birth is recorded only in the parish register. FamilySearch collection 1554443 'Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935' is indexed with 19 million records and covers this parish. A baptismal entry would directly name the father — primary evidence resolving the objective in one step. Search on given name Jan/Ján, surname Gallo, place Šútovo/Suttó/Turány, year 1893.

items.jurisdiction: Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary (now Turany, Martin district, Slovakia)
items.rationale: Per GPS parentage standards, the marriage record of Jan Gallo's parents must be searched independently. The parents' marriage in the Turány parish register would supply the father's full name, the mother's maiden name, and — by its date relative to the 1893 birth — corroborate the parentage link. Same collection 1554443. Search surname Gallo for marriages in Turány, approximate window 1875–1895.

items.jurisdiction: Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary (now Turany, Martin district, Slovakia)
items.rationale: FAN item: search for sibling baptisms of Jan Gallo (other children of the same father) in the Turány register, roughly 1885–1900. Sibling entries would independently name the father, establishing the family unit and corroborating any finding from item 1. Also helps distinguish between two men of the same name. Same collection 1554443; search surname Gallo, place Šútovo/Turány.

MyHeritage 'Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1910' (collection 30139) indexes the same underlying Slovak church records as FamilySearch. If the FamilySearch index is missing the 1893 Gallo baptism or the handwriting was transcribed differently, MyHeritage may surface it with a variant spelling. Fallback for item 1.

FamilySearch collection 1986782 'Slovakia, Census, 1869' covers this area (278,392 images; browse-only, 0% indexed). Jan Gallo was not yet born, but his father would appear as a young man or boy in the Šútovo/Suttó household. Finding a Gallo household head or family in the 1869 census would help identify which Gallo man was of the right age to be the father in 1893, and supply his parents' names (Jan's grandparents). Browse the Suttó/Turány pages. Fallback if church records are unavailable or ambiguous.

log.notes: Top match: Jan Gallo, Baptism 1893, Turany, Martin, Slovakia (ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R). Match score 0.464 — large gap over rank 2 (0.084). Place and year match exactly. attachedToOther:true. Proceeding to read full record.

40 results returned but subjectResolvable:false (subject too sparse) and all match scores ~0.00014 — no clear Turány hit. Most results are Andreae Gallo appearing as witness not as groom. Retrying with isPrincipal:true and marriagePlace Turany.

12 results for Andrej/Ondrej Gallo marriages at Turány. Read top 6 candidates; spouses were: Mária Milan (1887), Susanna Augustin (1866), Anna Ulicky (1874), Anna Janik (1905), Susana Prindeš (1868), Judyta Bálint (1883). None married Katarina Križko. The Gallo–Križko marriage is likely in the unindexed portion of the register. Primary evidence (baptismal record log_001) already directly names father as Andrej Gallo. No additional marriage-record corroboration found in indexed records.

Sibling corroboration found. Ondrej Gallo (1895, Turany) — father: Ondrej/Andrej Gallo, mother: Katarina Kricko (variant of Križko) — same couple as Jan Gallo 1893 baptism. Source: Hungary, Turócz, Religious, 1653–1951. Katarina Gallo 1897 (Turany) has mother Katarina Carácz — different Gallo family, not a sibling of Jan. Key sibling record: ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS (Ondrej Gallo 1895).

assertions.informant_bias_notes: No explicit marriage statement in the baptismal register. The couple relationship is inferred from their joint listing as father and mother of the baptized child. They may have been married, but the record does not assert it.

assertions.value: child of Ondrej Gallo (father, inferred from register structure)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Parentage inferred from the ParentChild relationship recorded in the baptismal register; the register structure (not a stated column) establishes the link. Father's name 'Ondrej' is a Slovak variant of 'Andrej' (both forms of Andrew).

assertions.value: child of Katarina Kricko (mother, inferred from register structure)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Parentage inferred from the ParentChild relationship recorded in the baptismal register. Mother's surname 'Kricko' is a spelling variant of 'Križko', the same surname recorded for Jan Gallo's mother in the 1893 baptism.
'Ondrej' is a Slovak variant of 'Andrej' (both are forms of Andrew). Same name-form as the father named in Jan Gallo's 1893 baptism in the same Turány parish.
'Kricko' is a spelling variant of 'Križko', the same mother's surname recorded in the 1893 baptism of Jan Gallo from the same Turány parish register.

person_evidence.rationale: Assertion names 'Jan Gallo' as the baptized child in the 1893 Turány register entry (ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R). This record was retrieved by searching specifically for Jan Gallo born ~1893 in Turány — the Obvious category. Name, year (1893), and parish (Turány/Turany) match exactly the known subject.

Christening date (1893) and place (Turany, Martin, Slovakia) directly match the known subject Jan Gallo. The record was retrieved by a targeted search for this specific individual; officiating priest recorded the event contemporaneously.

Assertion names 'Andrej Gallo' as father in the baptismal record of the identified subject Jan Gallo. The father role in a contemporaneous parish baptismal entry is unambiguous. I1 was minted directly from this persona with no competing candidate — the record names exactly one father. Name, surname, and parish are consistent with I1's profile.

Assertion names 'Katarina Križko' as mother in the 1893 baptismal entry of identified subject Jan Gallo. I2 was minted directly from this persona. The mother role in a contemporaneous Catholic register is unambiguous; only one mother is named.

Relationship assertion places the baptized child as 'child of Andrej Gallo' in the 1893 Turány register. The baptized persona has been confidently identified as Jan Gallo (PID-TODO). Assertion corroborates the ParentChild relationship R2.

This same relationship assertion names Andrej Gallo as the father of the baptized child. I1 (Andrej Gallo) is directly implicated as the father-side of the relationship recorded in the Turány 1893 baptism.
Relationship assertion places the baptized child as 'child of Katarina Križko'. The baptized persona is PID-TODO (Jan Gallo). Corroborates ParentChild relationship R3.
This relationship assertion names Katarina Križko as mother of the baptized child. I2 (Katarina Križko) is the mother-side of the relationship in the 1893 Turány baptism.

Inferred spouse relationship between Andrej Gallo and Katarina Križko, derived from their joint listing as father and mother in the 1893 baptism. The register does not explicitly record a marriage; the couple relationship is inferred from the record structure. Probable rather than confident because the assertion itself is classified indeterminate/indirect.
Same inferred couple assertion bears on I2 (Katarina Križko). Probable because the spousal link is inferred from co-listing as parents, not stated explicitly in the register.

Assertion names 'Ondrej Gallo' as the baptized child in the 1895 Turány register (ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS). I3 was minted from this persona. The record was retrieved by searching for children of Andrej Gallo in Turány 1882–1902; name (Ondrej Gallo), year (1895), place (Turany), and both parents (Ondrej/Andrej Gallo + Katarina Kricko/Križko) match exactly. No competing sibling candidate with the same name and year was found.
Christening date (1895) and place (Turany, Martin, Slovakia) directly apply to I3 (Ondrej Gallo, sibling of Jan). Recorded contemporaneously by the officiating priest.

Inferred relationship placing Ondrej Gallo (I3) as child of the father_of_baptized persona. The parentage is established from the register's structural column recording of father and child, not a direct verbal assertion. Probable because evidence type is indirect.
This inferred relationship assertion also names I1 (Andrej/Ondrej Gallo) as the father of Ondrej Gallo (I3) in the 1895 Turány baptism. Corroborates the ParentChild relationship R4.
Inferred relationship placing I3 (Ondrej Gallo) as child of mother Katarina Kricko (I2). Established from register structure; evidence type is indirect.
This inferred relationship assertion also names I2 (Katarina Križko / Kricko) as mother of Ondrej Gallo (I3) in the 1895 Turány baptism. Corroborates ParentChild relationship R5.

Assertion names 'Ondrej Gallo' as father_of_baptized in the 1895 Turány register (sibling of Jan). 'Ondrej' is the documented variant of 'Andrej' (both Slovak forms of Andrew). Combined with the mother Katarina Kricko (= Križko), the family unit matches I1's profile exactly. This is independent corroboration from a second original register entry, two years after the 1893 Jan Gallo baptism.

Assertion names 'Katarina Kricko' as mother_of_baptized in the 1895 Turány register. 'Kricko' is a documented spelling variant of 'Križko'. Combined with father Ondrej/Andrej Gallo (I1), the family unit corroborates I2's identity. Independent confirmation from a second original register entry.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: FamilySearch collection 1554443 ('Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935') was searched for Jan Gallo's own baptism (positive: ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R, 1893, Turany), for the parents' marriage (negative: twelve Andrej/Ondrej Gallo marriages at Turány examined, none matched Katarina Križko as spouse; marriage likely in unindexed portion of register), and for sibling baptisms as FAN corroboration (positive: Ondrej Gallo, 1895, ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS). MyHeritage fallback (pli_004) and a browse-only 1869 census (pli_005) were identified as contingency items and were not needed given the direct evidence found. Research declared reasonably exhaustive; overturn risk is low.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Proof Summary: Father of Jan Gallo (1893, Šútovo, Turány Parish, Slovakia)

**Research Question:** What does the Turány parish baptismal register record for Jan Gallo, born 1893 in Šútovo (Suttó), Turócz County, say about his father?

**Conclusion:** The evidence proves that Andrej Gallo, of Šútovo (Suttó), Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary (now Turany, Martin district, Slovakia), is the father of Jan Gallo, baptized 1893 in the same parish.

### Research Scope

Hungary did not introduce civil registration until October 1895; Jan Gallo's 1893 birth is therefore recorded only in the parish register. FamilySearch collection 1554443 ("Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935"), the primary indexed repository for this region and period, was searched for: the subject's own baptism (positive); the parents' marriage (negative — not found in indexed records); and sibling baptisms as FAN corroboration (positive). No unexamined record type is likely to name a different father; overturn risk is low.

### Primary Source: Jan Gallo's 1893 Baptism

The 1893 Turány Catholic parish baptismal entry for Jan Gallo (accessed via FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R, "Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935") directly names "Andrej Gallo" as father and "Katarina Križko" as mother.¹ This is an **original source** — a digital image of the contemporaneously recorded parish register — carrying **primary information** supplied by the presenting parents (firsthand witnesses) and recorded by the officiating priest. The naming of the father is **direct evidence**: no inference is required.

### Corroborating Source: Ondrej Gallo's 1895 Sibling Baptism

The 1895 Turány Catholic parish baptismal entry for Ondrej Gallo (accessed via FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS, same collection) records the same family unit: father "Ondrej Gallo," mother "Katarina Kricko."² This is a second original source with primary information from a structurally separate record-creation event — a different baptism, two years later, for a different child.

Two name variants appear across the entries. "Ondrej" (1895) and "Andrej" (1893) are both standard Slovak vernacular forms of Andrew, used interchangeably by scribes in this region. "Kricko" (1895) and "Križko" (1893) are phonetic/scribal variants of the same surname, common in Slovak/Hungarian registers of the period.

A potential concern is whether the 1893 "Andrej Gallo" and the 1895 "Ondrej Gallo" are the same individual, since the marriage search (log_003) revealed twelve different Andrej/Ondrej Gallo entries at Turány — indicating that Gallo men of this name were not rare in the parish. That ambiguity is resolved by the mother's identity: among those twelve marriages, not one records Katarina Križko as the bride. The specific pairing of father Andrej/Ondrej Gallo with mother Katarina Križko/Kricko appears in only these two baptismal entries, affirmatively eliminating namesake collision as an explanation for the corroborating 1895 entry.

Both records' father-name information ultimately derives from the same presenting parents at two separate baptisms. While the two entries are structurally distinct documentary acts, they do not constitute fully independent informant chains in the strict GPS sense. This is an inherent limitation of baptismal-only evidence, which could not be resolved further because the Gallo–Križko marriage record remains in the unindexed portion of the register. The two entries nonetheless constitute strong, separate-event corroboration of paternity.

### Marriage Record

A targeted search for the Gallo–Križko marriage in the indexed portion of collection 1554443 examined twelve Andrej/Ondrej Gallo marriages at Turány; none matched Katarina Križko as spouse. The marriage likely resides in the unindexed portion of the register. This absence does not affect the parentage conclusion, which rests on two direct, primary, original baptismal entries that agree on both parents.

### Conclusion

The totality of evidence proves that **Andrej Gallo is the father of Jan Gallo, baptized 1893 in Šútovo, Turány parish, Turócz County, Hungary.** The subject's 1893 baptism directly names Andrej Gallo as father (original source, primary information, direct evidence). The 1895 baptism of sibling Ondrej Gallo independently names the same couple, and the fact that none of the twelve other indexed Gallo marriages at Turány records a Katarina Križko as bride eliminates the risk of namesake collision. Both records were made contemporaneously by the officiating priest. The name variants Andrej/Ondrej and Križko/Kricko are documented and explained. No conflicting evidence was found. All five GPS components are satisfied; research is declared reasonably exhaustive.

---
¹ "Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-N5R : accessed 2026-07-21), Entry for Jan Gallo and Andrej Gallo, 1893; digital image of Turány Catholic parish baptismal register, Hungary, Turócz, Religious registers, 1653–1951.

² "Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books, 1592–1935," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:KHWD-XGS : accessed 2026-07-21), Entry for Ondrej Gallo and Ondrej Gallo, 1895; digital image of Turány Catholic parish baptismal register, Hungary, Turócz, Religious registers, 1653–1951.
